threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the Tube app before every journey instead of just embracing the bus as your new best mate.
Last night on the 333, a guy serenaded the whole bus with 'Ain't No Mountain High Enough.' Even the driver joined in! Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw an older man arguing with a pigeon over a bread roll outside the bakery. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you get a public transport strike that ends just in time for rush hour. It's like a bizarre choreography no one asked for.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only true locals know to avoid BART at rush hour when the brakes overheat. Tourists line up while we just take the bus or bike.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy pull a full-on opera solo on the 31 while juggling his lunch and bag. Didn't even miss a note when the driver hit a bump.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our streets are always bustling, but after 10 PM, Gràcia turns into a quiet village—perfect for those who know the secret spots.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they still take photos with their phone in front of every single Gaudí building. It’s like a walking tour on repeat.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' and don’t know the secret bus routes that locals swear by during strikes.
Last night on the 196, a guy was narrating his life story to no one in particular. It was intense—like a one-man show on wheels. Quite the ride, I must say.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the U-Bahn was packed with a mix of people singing loudly, while the busker in the corner played 'Wonderwall' on repeat. Classic Neukölln vibe!

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find a U-Bahn station that doubles as a secret rave spot after hours. It's like a club buried beneath the city!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals can spot the real burrito spots by how they order: no green sauce, and definitely no 'to-go' for a sit-down meal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a sax in a crowded bus, next stop he gets up, finishes his solo, and everyone just goes back to scrolling their phones. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every 'bar' means tapas, but half the time they’re just serving vermut and dodgy pinchos. It’s all about the vibe, not just the food.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitation at the tram stops, trying to decipher the confusing route maps while clutching a guidebook tighter than a lifebuoy.
